name: Coffee Gallery
tagline: North Shore roastery with Oahu, Maui, and Kona beans — the real locals' coffee stop in Haleiwa.
parking: Parking in the North Shore Marketplace lot — easy
talkStory: They've been a Haleiwa fixture for years and the staff knows every regular by name and order. They roast beans from multiple Hawaiian estates: Waialua (grown right here on Oahu's North Shore), Maui, Kona, and Kauai. The Waialua estate is on the Dole Plantation side of the North Shore. Grab a bag of Waialua beans as a souvenir — it's genuinely local and most visitors don't know Oahu grows coffee. The Waialua estate coffee is the move — you're literally drinking coffee grown 10 miles from where you're sitting. Good pastry case too. Great first stop before a North Shore beach day.
